page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 30 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 15 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 120 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench text processing tests scoring in the top 25%. Memory performance is also strong, as reflected in the PassMark memory score. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth is measured at 71.86 GB/sec for reads and 25.49 GB/sec for writes. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small 135M models, while medium 7B model inference and database operations perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-standard-30 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for high-speed local database logging or heavy parallel graphics processing. The instance is best suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30 is a general-purpose server configured with 30 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware features 15 physical cores with hyper-threading, 32 MB of L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing, alongside strong PassMark memory scores. Multi-core performance, database operations, and uncached memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, though small-model LLM text generation reaches the top 10%. This resource profile represents a balanced, cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads, though the absence of local NVMe storage is a notable tradeoff. It is suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing tasks.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.

Strong single-core CPU and memory performance ranking in the top 25%
